w:r.w:t: People in. One quick second. need to change this. Everybody hear me okay?
Hey, Jeff. Hi, Suzanne. Can you hear me?
I can hear you, Jeff. Yep. Hi, guys.

All right. So, um, Josh, uh, Suzanne, Suzanne, Josh, Josh is helping us build this app. Um, what we wanted to do was just be able to let Josh pick your brain a little bit, um, today on the process and different things.
And let me share something and I'm going to try to get you to this, Josh, later this week, but, um, what I'm trying to build for you is, um, in, to, my um, uh,
And Figma, just a kind of a process flow and edits for the screen. Perfect.
Appreciate it. We this, we want to do this, you know, and I'm about to paste this one in here for the workshop registrations and what I want to do there.
So I want to keep marking this up for you so you can kind of see where our head's at and work with you as well when I get at least a skeleton built here of this.
Of what we want to do. So for instance, like in my brain here, after they log in, if it's a coach login, you know, I want just a couple of things here, their dashboard, their bio.

The dashboard is really just all of their workshops, a button to create a new one. The links for the registration should go to some sort of registration so I can unregister them, see who's registered the list.
Probably have a button up here that says export list, stuff like that. So I'll document all. So Thank All stuff and the flow that I see, and hopefully they'll help with some of your questions.
But I wanted to show you, first make sure that this was going to work for you as far as helping you at all.
If it's not going to help you, I'm not going to waste my time.
It is, Jeff. It is a big help because currently in my process of documenting or in process of context engineering, currently my AI doesn't have capability of ingesting a video.
So it's currently ingesting time step, time skips, plus screenshots. So this would definitely help. So thank you for doing that.
Okay. So I hope to get to this in a day or two. So I'm going to let you lead at this point, Josh, to be able to ask whatever questions you need from Suzanne.
All right. So Suzanne, let me just walk you through a bit. Let just share my screen or yeah, let me just share my screen.
I think that would be faster.
Okay. I apologize if things are loading a quick slow here. So this is the current dashboard. I believe Jeff gave you the link.
If not, I'm going to just share this here in the chat. Yep. So if you can open it on, if you have another screen, so you can sort of give you a detail.
So when you see the screen here, you have go to the dashboard here. So ideally, what would happen is if you get into a window.
just so just real quick, I believe, Suzanne, you've already kind of clicked around in here and set up a sample of a meeting.
you're going So You already, didn't you? Yeah, I was playing around in there.
And so any feedback or any critical things that you want to put in something like a contact section for the coaches or anything that would help you be more efficient in terms of your workflow here?
I'm trying to piece out the UI, and I would like to know your opinions.

There is a couple things. The coaches will need like a discount code option because they'll periodically want to be able to give like a discount to somebody, like for example, like a sponsor that wants to send somebody from their team.

We need to be able to give them like 100% discount or something so that they can register so we still have their contact information that a human, another body is coming to the register or going to be attending.
But. I But they don't have to pay for it. So if there's a way we could add like a discount code option in there.
So you mean whenever we create a registration here, so just to be clear and just to be on record as well, you meant here, discount option here?
So that they have a code that they can create. So a coach can create a code and then give that code to whoever they want to give that code to.
Okay. Just a couple of questions here. How does the discount work? Can you walk me through how?
So let's say a workshop is $500 and they want to discount that code or they want to discount that workshop for certain people to like say $250 or even free for some people.

So they might want to give a code to somebody. So when that person's checking out, they would put in the code like workshop100, and then that code would tell the checkout, take 100% off of that, or whatever code name they gave that, it would take a discount and discount that workshop for that person checking out.
ACTION ITEM: Research Stripe coupons; confirm coach-level multi-use; propose 25/50/100% codes -
Perfect. So some kind of like a voucher code, like you can just ingest it, and then it would automatically discount the price.

Correct. And Josh, my guess is you're going to have to handle that through Stripe, because Stripe Handle does coupons as well, so you can create the coupons in Stripe, and then when they do the checkout, they would apply that coupon, and I'm assuming we're going to use Stripe for the checkout.
It would all be tied together.
Yeah, 100%. Now, let me just do a quick research on how we didn't do that on Stripe, Jeff, but I would definitely suggest give that suggestions, yes.

Well, and just talking that out a little bit more. Yeah. Would the coach, Jeff, when you're saying him doing that through Stripe, would a coach have the ability, like for example, this just happened, where Jeff Donaldson, one of our coaches, they have something coming up.
He sent me, he said, can I add in these like three discount codes because he's doing three different like potential discounts for different reasons that he has.
Would a coach be able to modify and like create their own discounts for their workshop? Like not just a generic, like every coach has like a hundred off.
So what you're trying to question here is if they have a custom coupon voucher code for every discount code, right?
For every discount that they want to give for different workshops. Is that correct?
Right. Let me research on that. Again, I can't really definitely answer on that, Suzanne, because I'm not really quite certain on how Stripe works or how voucher code works on...
And Josh, if you want to look at it, if you're in Kajabi, you can go in there and you can see the coupons.
So it's under sales, I believe. And then coupons.
And it kind of shows you the functionality she's using. Right now, in the current setup, the coaches don't have access to any of this.
This is all backend admin stuff. So like if you do new coupon, yeah, you can see it. And you can create one.
So just a quick question here.
Would you like it to be some sort of a UI where you can just create a coupon just like this, just embed it directly?

And the bulk codes, I think you can use multiple times or something like that. I can't remember.
Okay. So actually, can you walk me through how this works just to imprint it on my mind?
Like for example, when you go. So just do, Suzanne, just do a single code. Like if we were going to do 100% off an event so they can invite like whoever they wanted to for free.
Yeah. Do a single coupon. And then we would put a coupon code like Detroit 60, Detroit 60, and then percent off.
And that would probably be 60% off because you put Detroit 60, just to try and make it make sense with the percentage.
Okay. Okay. And then expiration date here.
Okay. So you also have, you know, how many times?
Yeah. So no, it would just be once. You know, some of these, you know, this is built for different things and we're just doing workshops.
So it would be a one-time use for that. Okay. Okay. So I'll press continue here.
Like I said, you're not going to hurt anything.
We can delete it when you're done. Yeah.

You know, we might want it to be done just once for an offer, or we might want to be able to be able to, that code to be used four and five and six times or whatever, and specify how many times that coupon can be used.
Okay. A quick clarification here. For a single, whenever they send Detroit 60 to one of their peers, because this coupon is going to be sent for their peers, right?
Or for the people who are going to be, yep. And so for the single here, this is just a single use for every person, right?
That would sign on through the workshop. Okay. All right.
Yeah. So Susanna, and I'm going from memory here, so correct me if I'm wrong. I thought single meant once it's used, it's done, nobody can use it again.
Yeah. I know. As he was asking that, I don't, I think it's per person, but I could all, I don't know for sure, to be honest.

Let us research that for you, Josh, and I'll explain the functionality we're looking for. But in essence here, that's what we need to be able to do is do a custom coupon that affects the payment process so we can discount either a portion or completely.

And, you know, are we going to reuse this coupon or do we have to create? If I, for instance, if we needed to do a half-off coupon, would we create 10 of them and once the 10 of them were done, fine, or do we allow it to be reused 10 times, right?
So there's two ways to solve that. And Jeff, another way that it's probably going to be used is the AI workshops.
You can bring a team of three, let's say, to it. Okay, so your one ticket will cover three people, but an owner might want to send another group of three where we might.
Might give them a 50% off discount for the second group of three or something. So that one owner is signing up, might be signing up multiple people, so it would need to be probably used.
And how are we doing that now? Right now, I've done it one time with Jeff. I'm trying to think how I did that.
I created a second offer. So in the checkout page, they could click a link to add more people, and then that was like a new price.
I didn't do it as a discount code, but...

So it's a good So the, let me just create a quick one here.
Yes, we talked about a few of these things.
Let's just go over real quick, Josh. Okay.

So the coach is going to log in. It's going to go to their coach's page, their coach's dashboard page, right?

Yeah, to make our lives easier.
Yeah. And then they just click whatever coupon code that they want.
Yeah. And I'm almost thinking you just do, like you said, 25, 50, 100, and call it a day.

Yeah. Yes, that would definitely work as well. Yeah. Let me research on the Circle token data passing of how it works.

I'm using, I'm, I'm, using Neon. So what I'm, I'm suggesting here is just put all the data from the active coaches.
We do have an active database from the coaches on Circle, right?
And can. There's users in Circle with all of our coaches.
It's in there. It's also in HubSpot.

All the coaches are listed in there as well. I'm just saying if we're treating this like any other application in the world, that application would have some sort of authentication mechanism built into it, right?

With the ability to change their passwords, reset their passwords, forgot their password, all that functionality. I'm just saying if we can leverage something that's already in use by the coaches, that's one less thing we have to build.
It's just, you know, verifying authentication at that point.
Okay. Gotcha. Gotcha. So let me research on that. If we can use the authentication from Circle so that we don't have to implement it here, right?

that. Go back to the template. At the very end of this, think, scroll down.

And you could pick which landing page you wanted to send them to.
So the flow is you've got a landing page.
Inside the landing page is the registration purchase block, right? And then after they complete the purchase, it's going to go to a landing page that we specify.

Yep. Yep, yep. Only with the admin side of things.
Only with the admin side. So Suzanne can go in there and build her templates for the offers for the landing pages and for the thank you pages.

And just to try to keep our terminology the same when we're talking about this. Let's call the one, landing page, the piece that's where you're making the payment, we're going to call that the offer, and then the thank you page.
The thank you page. All right. Okay, so anything else here?
Not off the top of my head that I can think.
Well, do you want to talk about the surveys, Jeff?
Oh, this is a piece we haven't talked about yet, Josh. This part of, is it both the workshops, Exit and AI, Suzanne?
Yeah. So in those survey, in those workshops, we have surveys that we've built right now in type form, I think you said?
Now it doesn't have to be in type form. We can do it within here, or we can continue to point them to type form surveys.
But there are survey information that goes out, what, before the workshop happens?
And then a kind of a, how did you Would you like it survey afterwards? Is that correct? Correct.
So we need the ability to either embed those surveys in there. If we continue to use Typeform, we can do that.
And they're just links that go out. that's really part of the process. But in the form setup of these workshops, we need to be able to say, include these surveys.
Yep. But this is after the workshop, right?
No, before somebody registers to join the workshop, they get a thank you email, or like you're registered. Congratulations, you've registered with the information of the workshop.
And then in there, we ask them to complete. It's a pre-assessment prior to the workshop. And if there's a way we could get that out of Typeform and do it in here, that would be ideal.
Because what's happening right now is... I have to take the survey and duplicate it and make it specific to each coach.
gets its own URL so that that way when somebody's filling out the form for this coach and this workshop, I have the comments and the answers for that to give to the coach.
This coach has their own. This coach has their own. So I'm having to go in and duplicate for each event, if that makes sense, its own survey.
So in order to restrict right now with type form, which event and which coach is there, she has to create a unique type form event.
If we did something more like in this tool as a different module, if you will, you know, you would have a unique identifier for each one, right?

That's, okay, it's this event and this coach. And then as a coach, when I'm looking at my workshop, I could have a button that says survey data, and it would go in there and show me everybody that's filled out the surveys and their answers.
The questions for the surveys, would it be different for every workshop?
Would it be different for... No, it's the same.
All the exit surveys have the same exact questions, and all the AI surveys have the same exact questions.

And then the bigger stuff that we can't do now that would be then nice is then if we had them all in the system, then I could start looking for trends amongst all of the surveys and all of the stuff in a single view, so we can then apply maybe some AI logic to it and say, what are the trends on these answers?
What are the trends on these answers? And be able to do stuff with that data. Now, that's future, future, future us.
But that's why if we can build it in here, and it doesn't seem like that would be a huge ask for just to be able to do some questions in a form.
On here. But, you know, now we're asking you to rebuild Typeform on top of rebuilding Kajabi, which I know that let's take this in stages.
ACTION ITEM: Add Typeform integration; store responses; enable coach view -
So short term, I'm content with continuing to do what we have to do with, well, is that true? Because we can't.

If we're trying to make this fully automated, I can't have Suzanne creating unique Typeforms for each customer. So if we're not going to build it in here, we're going to have to be able to do some sort of an integration to Typeforms.
So we do that. And at that point, I'm guessing it's easier just to recreate what we want in here.
Yeah, I think there's a feature where we can integrate Typeform inside here as well to make it easier.
And it's just, to me, then, it's a question of, and it sounds like you do have a data repository in here.
It's just another repository. Dispository of data. Yep. It's possible, Jeff.

So I will add that functionality into our list here. And remember, the other piece to this as well that we don't have here is being able to upload materials that then you can assign materials to the courses, to the workshops.
So like the workshop has a workbook that goes with it, right? And at some point during the process, we're sending the people who sign up a copy of the work, we're not?
No, we're not. So I don't even think we need to do that because they only get a workbook if they show up in person.
If you wanted to down the road, Jeff, modify how we do that or something, I think we could send something as like a hyperlink to a PDF, like inside of an email.
I don't think that. that. I would just be an email, and we could put that in Google Drive or wherever we wanted to put that.
It wouldn't have to be here.
Yeah, agreed. Agreed. All right. All righty. So I think, Jeff, what I would need moving forward are the Figma and anything else in here, really, that would make it convenient on your end, Suzanne.
I know we would be approaching the updates for FACES, and you've already given me so much critical feedback that I also have to assess.
Is there anything else, really, on the workshop setup checklist here that I haven't covered or I haven't really put in as a function?
Yeah. So I think the two big ones were the discount codes and the survey pieces were the two big edits that we had.
The rest is kind of manipulating some stuff here, and then the agreement that we had that this is really designed for...
For an admin functionality, as well as a coach functionality.
As a divider, if you will.
And the add of the potential buttons for the partner logos for that.
Oh, yeah. Partner logos as well. Yep. I do have a quick question here. For the marketing promo kit, what was it for?
No, don't even worry about that. They, you won't even, that's the process anymore.
Okay, okay. All right, all right. Okay. So, should we conclude our call here or should, and if there's anything else that you would like to move forward, Jeff?
ACTION ITEM: Share Figma w/ Josh; send access; build skeleton; share before Fri -
I don't think so. I think our next meeting is Friday, right? Friday. So, that'll give you a couple of days to at least get your head wrapped around a few things here.
And I'm hoping by Friday, before Friday, hopefully, I'm going to give you at least a mock-up in Figma for you.
That would really be helpful, Jeff. I don't use Figma very often. And is there a way for me to add you to this so you can just grab it or have you used it?
there is. I don't use Figma as much because I'm not a designer, but I do know that there is a functionality that you can just give me a memory.
Okay, I'll figure that out and I'll send you a login to this.
As I edit this, you can just keep seeing what I'm putting in there.
Appreciate it, Jeff. And Suzanne, thank you for your time.
Yes, I'm just curious. No pressure because I know we're running. Yes. running, but what are we thinking for ETA of this to drop this?
Well, for the duration of the project, really, Suzanne, I want to be honest as I can. I want to do an iterative loop.
So basically, I'll be reporting to you every week. And the ETA for the entire project, because this would be an iterative loop, is indefinite for now.
I can't really say this would be in the next two weeks. In the next. I do know that by March, you would be needing the system, but we're mid into February, so we have four weeks until March, yes?
And so I will do my best, but by March, we could launch the entire application, really.
Our goal is going to be March 4, is it going to be, let's just call it March 9.
Let's march to that date, and get as much as we can done by that point, even if not all the functionality there, we can get a lot of it, that would be useful.
100%, because Chris also put me in here, because he wanted this to be as fast as possible, and so I will do my best, really, and I do, I do really don't want to have promises with.
So I will just be saying this to you guys that I will do my best on providing the timeline for March, okay?
All right. Josh, appreciate it as always, sir. You have a good one, and we will see you on Friday.
I appreciate your time as well. Thank you, Suzanne. Thank you, Jeff. Have a great day. Yeah. Thanks, Jeff.
